name: Beast Ball
effect: A special Poké Ball designed to catch Ultra Beasts. It has a low success rate for catching others.

name: Cherish Ball
effect: Rre Poké Ball used for event Pokémon.

name: Dive Ball
effect: Catches Pokémon that live in water with 3.5x the rate of a standard Poké Ball.

name: Dream Ball
effect: A special Poké Ball that appears out of nowhere in a bag at the Entree Forest. It can catch any Pokémon.

name: Dusk Ball
effect: Catches Pokémon at night or in caves with 3x the rate of a standard Poké Ball.

name: Fast Ball
effect: A Poké Ball that makes it easier to catch Pokémon which are quick to run away.

name: Friend Ball
effect: A Poké Ball that makes caught Pokémon more friendly.

name: Great Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon with 1.5x the rate of a Poké Ball.

name: Heal Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon and fully heals them.

name: Heavy Ball
effect: A Poké Ball for catching very heavy Pokémon.

name: Level Ball
effect: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are a lower level than your own.

name: Love Ball
effect: Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that are the opposite gender of your Pokémon.

name: Lure Ball
effect: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon hooked by a Rod when fishing.

name: Luxury Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon and increases their friendship more quickly.

name: Master Ball
effect: Catches any wild Pokémon without fail.

name: Moon Ball
effect: A Poké Ball for catching Pokémon that evolve using the Moon Stone.

name: Nest Ball
effect: Catches lower level wild Pokémon with a higher rate (up to 4x for level 1).

name: Net Ball
effect: Catches Water- and Bug-type Pokémon with 3.5x the rate of a standard Poké Ball.

name: Park Ball
effect: A special Poké Ball for the Pal Park.

name: Poké Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon.

name: Premier Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon; received after buying 10 Poké Balls.

name: Quick Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon with a higher rate (5x) on the first turn of a battle.

name: Repeat Ball
effect: Catches Pokémon already registered in the Pokédex with 3.5x the rate of a standard Poké Ball.

name: Safari Ball
effect: Catches Pokémon in the Safari Zone or Great Marsh.

name: Sport Ball
effect: A special Poké Ball for the Bug-Catching Contest.

name: Timer Ball
effect: Catches Pokémon with a higher rate the more turns have occurred (up to 4x).

name: Ultra Ball
effect: Catches wild Pokémon with 2x the rate of a Poké Ball.
